steps.title: Match the conclusion
steps.content: The team concluded that anatomical features may affect dogs' ability to distinguish speech from nonspeech. Supporting evidence must connect a body feature to a difference in that exact speech-nonspeech distinction.

steps.title: Compare head-shape results
steps.content: A compares familiar languages within long-headed dogs, C tests familiar versus unfamiliar speech, and D compares mismatched categories. A smaller speech-nonspeech brain-activity difference in shorter-headed dogs directly links anatomy to the ability. The answer is B.
